title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Execution Trace: Feed Go Secret Configuration
summary: Security is split across ad hoc checks, admin paths bypass URL policy, XML and HTML have different budgets, search supports denial of service, secrets enter logs, and data is kept forever. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go secret configuration increment in threat-modeled feed-service control set. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Load typed configuration from approved sources, separate environments, redact values, rotate credentials, and reject insecure defaults.
Align wire, decompression, XML, text, HTML sanitation, search, pagination, bulk, and export limits so one stage cannot amplify another.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Secret Configuration · Theory Feed Go Secret Configuration

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
